description: Added a new prototype family, `lowrank_jumprelu_residual`, by combining the existing low-rank residual trunk with JumpReLU-gated residual activations. Main risk: plain `jumprelu` was weak before, so the gain must come specifically from applying it only on the residual lane after the low-rank trunk.

description: Added a new prototype family, `lowrank_residual_vq`, in `sparsify/` and registered it in config/factory dispatch. The architecture applies the existing low-rank residual trunk first, then performs hard residual VQ on the trunk residual before sparse top-k correction. Exact smoke test passed for `architecture=lowrank_residual_vq, K=24, EF=12`, and a quick encode comparison confirmed behavior differs from `lowrank_residual`. This is a genuinely different mechanism family for severe-stagnation recovery, not a scalar sweep.
self_review: This satisfies the policy requirement to try a fundamentally different architecture after repeated no-improve rounds. The design is intentionally architecture-only at the pinned `K=24, EF=12, signum, LR=3.2e-3, AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` recipe so the result is attributable. Main risk: standalone discrete/codebook families have been weak before, so the win depends on the low-rank trunk making the codebook operate on an easier residual distribution rather than replaying the earlier failures.

description: Repaired `lowrank_multi_branch_residual` so the original round-60 experiment can run under DDP. The crash was caused by inherited `encoder.weight` and `encoder.bias` from `LowRankResidualSparseCoder` being unused in the new family, which triggered the prior-iteration reduction error. Patched `_mixed_acts()` so the inherited residual encoder is an always-on anchor branch alongside the routed branches, then re-ran the concrete factory-dispatch checks. Validation passed: all parameters now receive gradients, and the exact smoke target `architecture=lowrank_multi_branch_residual, K=24, EF=12` instantiates, runs forward, and backward successfully.
self_review: This is a repair only; the architecture name, hypothesis, and env overrides are unchanged. The fix addresses the precise DDP-unused-parameter blocker without redesigning the experiment. I also corrected the validation path by smoke-testing the actual architecture class through `_get_sae_class()` at the exact failing `(architecture, K, EF)` instead of relying on the base `SparseCoder` constructor.

description: Added a new prototype family, `lowrank_factorized_residual`, in `sparsify/` and registered it in config/factory dispatch. The architecture applies the existing low-rank trunk first, then replaces the direct residual-to-latent scoring path with a factorized bottleneck residual encoder before final top-k selection. Validation passed on the concrete dispatched architecture: exact smoke test succeeded for `architecture=lowrank_factorized_residual, K=24, EF=12`, and all parameters received gradients in a backward check, so this round stays on the architecture axis without repeating the last DDP blocker.
self_review: This satisfies the severe-stagnation policy by introducing a genuinely different residual mechanism rather than another scalar or K sweep. The experiment remains pinned to `K=24, EF=12, signum, LR=3.2e-3, AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`, so attribution stays clean. Main risk: plain `factorized_topk` previously lost badly at K=24, so the gain depends on moving factorization to the post-trunk residual regime rather than replaying the same failure in a different wrapper.

self_review: This satisfies the severe-stagnation policy by introducing a genuinely different residual mechanism rather than another K move or scalar tweak.
